name: Mi'kmaq

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Mi'kmaq (mikm1235).

notes: Skeleton. Algic (Algonquian) language of Atlantic Canada written in the Francis-Smith Roman orthography. Obstruents p t k q j s are voiceless here but are realized voiced intervocalically; that allophony is left unencoded. Acute-marked vowels denote length. The letter q represents a velar/uvular fricative [x]. Schwa and predictable stress are not consistently recovered from spelling, raising the PER floor.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Mi'kmaq (mikm1235)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
